checklistName: Post Merge Local Branch Cleanup Safety Checklist
cleanupPolicy: after merge, update main and confirm status/log/branch before considering local branch cleanup.
branchDeletePolicy: old feature branches may remain; delete only after confirming current branch is main and the target branch is already merged.

postMergeChecks.label: After merge, switch to main first.
Update main from the private repository.
Confirm the current branch and remaining local branches.

remainingBranchPolicy.label: Old local feature branches are not an immediate problem.
Do not automatically delete local branches.
Do not run dangerous bulk branch deletion.

deleteBeforeChecks.label: Before deletion, confirm the current branch is main.
Confirm the target branch is already merged.
Confirm the exact target branch name before deletion.

mainAheadResponse.label: If main is ahead, stop before any push.
Do not push main when ahead appears unexpectedly.
Use a feature branch or PR-safe recovery path instead of main push.

prohibitedActions.label: Do not make GitHub Pages required.

operationPrinciples: Cleanup starts with main update, working tree clean confirmation, log review, and branch review.
Local feature branches may remain after merge without causing an immediate problem.
Deletion is manual, explicit, and only after merge status is confirmed.
Private repository, local first, and no GitHub Pages dependency remain the operating premise.
nextRecommendedStep: After PR merge, run git switch main, git pull origin main, git status, git log --oneline -5, and git branch before considering any local branch cleanup.
